questions: thanda te angul jome gele ki korbo
extreme cold e skin er ki hoy
answers: Frostbite occurs when skin and tissue freeze due to extreme cold. It usually affects fingers, toes, nose, and ears. Signs include numbness, white/gray skin, and hard texture. Gradually rewarm the area in warm water (not hot).
Frostbite hole skin numb hoye jay, white ba gray color hoy, ar touch e hard feel hoy. Groom pani te (37-39°C) slowly warm korun. Rub korben na — tissue damage hote pare.
To treat frostbite: Get to warmth, soak affected area in warm water for 15-30 minutes, don't rub the area, take pain relievers if needed. Severe frostbite needs medical attention as tissue can die.
Frostbite theke bachte layers of clothing porun, gloves/socks pora dorkar, wet kapor avoid korun. Frostbite hole ghorshen na, direct heat deben na, ar doctor dekhano uchit karon gangrene hote pare.
